framing: Archived AIS playback plus offline-computed inference from committed research exports; not a live AIS backend or online optimizer.

framingPillars.title: Archived AIS playback is the scene clock
framingPillars.detail: Every module reuses the same committed historical harbor window instead of claiming a live stream.

framingPillars.title: Forecast, repair, clustering, and tuning all come from offline exports
framingPillars.detail: The website packages committed JSON and Plotly outputs from local research artifacts rather than rerunning notebooks in the browser.

framingPillars.title: This showcase is not a live AIS backend or online optimizer
framingPillars.detail: The interface can feel realtime, but its truth boundary remains archived playback plus offline-computed evidence with explicit deferred states.

businessLoop.description: Use committed historical AIS playback as the common scene clock for every later evidence surface.
Explain movement structure from raw to segmented, compressed, and corridor layers before other modules interpret traffic patterns.
Project near-future traffic change across the archived playback timeline using the committed multi-model forecast bundles.
Show how damaged sample tracks are reconstructed and compared against curated reference trajectories.
Bring forecast, repair, clustering context, and offline tuning evidence into one traceable cross-task evidence center.

Package rule-driven collaborative-decision scenarios on top of forecast, evaluation, and clustering evidence, with the interactive focus surface and before/after switching now shipped on top of the committed offline evidence.

moduleEntryPoints.label: Flow Prediction
moduleEntryPoints.summary: BiLSTM leads the shipped 1h RMSE ranking across 3 forecast models and 3 horizons.

moduleEntryPoints.label: Trajectory Repair
moduleEntryPoints.summary: 3 curated repair samples are shipped, with ATT-BILSTM currently leading the aggregate RMSE comparison.

moduleEntryPoints.label: Trajectory Clustering
moduleEntryPoints.summary: 16 runtime corridors and 1321 corridor tracks keep clustering connected to one dominant movement spine led by D07-C01.

moduleEntryPoints.label: Evaluation Center
moduleEntryPoints.summary: The evaluation center now combines forecast or repair rankings with 13 completed optimization checkpoints from the shipped offline tuning study.

moduleEntryPoints.label: Forward-Looking Analysis
moduleEntryPoints.summary: 4 curated BiLSTM 1h scenarios now connect focus-route decisions to evaluation authority and corridor dominance.

scenarioEntryPoints.label: Archived playback window
scenarioEntryPoints.summary: Start from the command-center replay rather than generic logs so every module shares the same archived scene clock.
scenarioEntryPoints.detail: 577 timeline frames and 3 forecast horizons are already packaged for the same historical harbor window.

scenarioEntryPoints.label: Forecast entry
scenarioEntryPoints.summary: Jump directly into the strongest shipped 1h ranking before comparing hotspot or route-level behavior.
scenarioEntryPoints.detail: Available models: STGCN, LSTM, BiLSTM. Supported horizons: 1h, 2h, 3h.

scenarioEntryPoints.label: Repair entry
scenarioEntryPoints.summary: Begin from the first curated damaged trajectory sample instead of a notebook-only experiment snapshot.
scenarioEntryPoints.detail: 3 curated samples ship with ATT-BILSTM currently leading aggregate RMSE.

scenarioEntryPoints.label: Corridor entry
scenarioEntryPoints.summary: Follow the dominant corridor family that now anchors the site-wide movement story.
scenarioEntryPoints.detail: 1321 runtime tracks are grouped into 16 shipped corridors.

scenarioEntryPoints.label: Evaluation entry
scenarioEntryPoints.summary: The evaluation center now holds both cross-task rankings and the shipped offline tuning story.
scenarioEntryPoints.detail: 13 completed checkpoints, best objective 2.666e-08, top importance Batch size.

scenarioEntryPoints.label: Decision entry
scenarioEntryPoints.summary: Open the curated decision layer to inspect focus-route scenarios tied back to forecast rankings and corridor dominance.
scenarioEntryPoints.detail: Current scenario pack covers C03, C08, C12, C14. Corridor context stays honest and CLUS-03 remains deferred because normalized_distances(60,90,0.03).pkl is still zero-byte.
